2603.14782.title: Information Asymmetry across Language Varieties: A Case Study on Cantonese-Mandarin and Bavarian-German QA
2603.14782.paper_summary: Large Language Models (LLMs) are becoming a common way for humans to seek knowledge, yet their coverage and reliability vary widely. Especially for local language varieties, there are large asymmetries, e.g., information in local Wikipedia that is absent from the standard variant. However, little is known about how well LLMs perform under such information asymmetry, especially on closely related languages. We manually construct a novel challenge question-answering (QA) dataset that captures knowledge conveyed on a local Wikipedia page, which is absent from their higher-resource counterparts-covering Mandarin Chinese vs. Cantonese and German vs. Bavarian. Our experiments show that LLMs fail to answer questions about information only in local editions of Wikipedia. Providing context from lead sections substantially improves performance, with further gains possible via translation. Our topical, geographic annotations, and stratified evaluations reveal the usefulness of local Wikipedia editions as sources of both regional and global information. These findings raise critical questions about inclusivity and cultural coverage of LLMs.
2603.14782.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLMs）正逐漸成為人類尋求知識的常見方式，但它們的覆蓋範圍和可靠性差異很大。<lb/>特別是對於地方語言變體，存在著巨大的不對稱性，例如，在地方維基百科中存在的資訊在標準變體中缺失。<lb/>然而，對於在這種資訊不對稱下，LLMs的表現如何，尤其是在密切相關的語言中，仍知之甚少。<lb/>我們手動構建了一個新穎的挑戰問題回答（QA）數據集，捕捉了在地方維基百科頁面上傳達的知識，而這些知識在其高資源對應版本中缺失——涵蓋普通話與廣東話以及德語與巴伐利亞語。<lb/>我們的實驗顯示，LLMs無法回答僅存在於地方維基百科版本中的資訊問題。<lb/>提供來自主要部分的上下文顯著提高了表現，通過翻譯還可能進一步提升。<lb/>我們的主題、地理註釋和分層評估揭示了地方維基百科版本作為區域和全球資訊來源的實用性。<lb/>這些發現引發了關於LLMs的包容性和文化覆蓋的重要問題。
2603.14782.author: Renhao Pei et.al.
2603.14782.authors: Renhao Pei, Siyao Peng, Verena Blaschke, Robert Litschko, Barbara Plank
